Group 2 – no visa required; passport must be valid at least 3 months (90 natural days) from intended entry date. Honduran nationals aged 18 and over must present a non-apostilled Police Clearance Certificate (Certificado de Policía Nacional) upon entry, unless they are merely transiting or hold a qualifying credential from US/Canada/Schengen/EU/UK/Iceland/Norway/Switzerland. Reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023 under bilateral agreement signed October 24, 2023.

visa_policy.notes: Costa Rica uses a four-group visa classification system per Directrices Generales de Visas de Ingreso y Permanencia para No Residentes (Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025). Group 1 (180-day visa-free, passport validity 1 day from intended entry): ~60 nationalities including USA, Canada, EU/Schengen, UK, Australia, Japan, South Korea, Israel, South Africa, Qatar, UAE, and Latin American countries. Group 2 (30-day visa-free, passport validity 3 months/90 natural days from intended entry): Pacific island states, CARICOM members not in Group 1, Central American countries El Salvador/Guatemala/Honduras/Belize, Russia, Turkey, Philippines, Malaysia, etc. Honduras reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023; Honduran nationals aged 18+ must present a non-apostilled police clearance certificate. Group 3 (consular visa required, 30-day max, USD 32, 30–60 days processing, passport validity 6 months): ~80+ countries including India, China, Colombia, Kenya, most African/Asian/Middle-Eastern nations. Group 4 (restricted visa, 30-day max extendable to 90, USD 52, up to 90 days processing, passport validity 6 months): ~23 high-risk countries. Credential exceptions for Groups 3 and 4 listed in conditional_access. No e-visa or visa-on-arrival system exists. Puerto Rico and Northern Mariana Islands were removed from visa_policy as they are US territories and their residents travel on US passports (covered under United States of America entry).

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa in any category including refugee/asylum and type C1/D combined; excludes US-only transit visas C1, C2, and C3

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid US multiple-entry visa in any category — including refugee/asylum and the combined C1/D crew visa — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Excluded: US visa types C1, C2, and C3 (transit-only). Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended Costa Rica entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Directrices Generales de Visas, Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025 (text verified directly on migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid US residence permit (any category including refugee and/or asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining validity).

credential.subtype: multiple-entry visa in any category including refugee/asylum

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Canadian multiple-entry visa in any category (including refugee/asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Canadian residence permit (any category including refugee and/or asylum) may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Minimum credential validity: 1 natural day from the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining validity).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Schengen Category C (short-stay) visa with multiple-entry authorisation exclusively may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Visa must be multiple-entry; minimum validity 1 natural day from the intended entry date; visa must not have an effective start date after the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Schengen Category D (national/long-stay) visa with multiple-entry authorisation exclusively may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Visa must be multiple-entry; minimum validity 1 natural day from the intended entry date; visa must not have an effective start date after the intended entry date. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable; stay cannot exceed remaining visa validity).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid residence permit issued by an EU member state — that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Document must be officially issued. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable). EU states listed on DGME page: Germany, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Czech Republic, Cyprus, Croatia, Denmark, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Sweden.

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum); covers Scotland, Wales, England, Northern Ireland

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid UK residence permit (covering Scotland, Wales, England, or Northern Ireland) — that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable). Source: DGME Alcance N° 148 (migracion.go.cr/visas/).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Icelandic residence permit — that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Norwegian residence permit — that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

credential.subtype: residence permit allowing multiple entries or with minimum 90 natural days validity (excluding refugee/asylum)

conditional_access.conditions: Groups 3 and 4 nationals holding a valid Swiss residence permit — that allows multiple entries OR has a minimum validity of 90 natural days from the intended entry date — may enter Costa Rica without a consular visa. Refugee and asylum categories are excluded. Maximum stay: 30 calendar days (extendable).

cbi.notes: Costa Rica does not have a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program that grants citizenship directly in exchange for investment or donation. Citizenship requires completing a full naturalization process after extended legal residency (5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans; 7 years for all others). No official CBI program found on migracion.go.cr or rree.go.cr.

programs.name: Inversionista (Investor) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 (published in Official Gazette La Gaceta No. 135, July 14, 2021). Minimum investment of USD 150,000 in one of the recognized categories: real estate, company shares (active businesses), SUGEVAL-authorized securities, venture capital funds, tourism infrastructure, or national interest projects. Investment must be held continuously. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years total residency (for most nationalities), or 5 years for Central Americans, Spaniards, and Latin Americans. Amount cited in Article 4 as 'not less than one hundred and fifty thousand US dollars (US$150,000.00)' — this was reduced from USD 200,000 under a sunset clause provision.

programs.name: Rentista (Fixed-Income) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Law No. 9996 and Executive Decree No. 37112. Requires stable foreign-source monthly income of at least USD 2,500 per month. Income must come from outside Costa Rica and be demonstrated via certified bank statements. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years (5 years for Latin Americans/Central Americans/Spaniards).

programs.name: Pensionado (Retiree) Temporary Residence
programs.notes: Governed by Ley General de Migración y Extranjería No. 8764 and Law 9996. Requires a lifetime pension of at least USD 1,000 per month from a competent authority (Social Security, military, retirement fund, annuity). Pension must be permanent. Temporary residence issued for 2 years, renewable. Path to citizenship via naturalization: 7 years (5 years for Latin Americans/Central Americans/Spaniards).

programs.name: Nómadas Digitales (Digital Nomad) – Temporary Stay Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who provide paid remote services to a natural person or legal entity located outside Costa Rica using digital or telecommunication technologies. Must earn minimum USD 3,000/month (USD 4,000/month if including dependents) from outside Costa Rica, documented over the prior year.
programs.notes: Established under General Law of Migration and Foreigners Article 256 and Law 10008 (Item 11 bis). Duration: 1 year, renewable for a further year. Government application fee: USD 100 (or equivalent in colones, paid to Bank of Costa Rica account 001-0242480-0). Health insurance covering full stay with minimum USD 50,000 coverage required. Applications via online TramiteYa platform or in person at DGME offices. Digital nomad permit holders are exempt from paying taxes and customs duties on electronic devices for professional use.

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1: Group 1 passport validity changed from '1 month' to '1 natural day from intended entry date'. The DGME official page (migracion.go.cr/visas/, accessed via Firecrawl June 2026) explicitly states 'Para los nacionales ubicados en el Primer Grupo, hasta 1 día'. The prior record sourced from the Costa Rican Embassy Kenya used the more conservative '1 month' figure; the DGME primary source overrides this.

CORRECTION 2: Honduras added to visa_free Group 2 (30 days, police clearance for 18+ required). The prior record noted in gaps that Honduras had moved back to Group 2. This has now been confirmed from the embassy Kenya page, Fragomen alert, and search results. Honduras was reinstated effective October 26, 2023.

CORRECTION 3: São Tomé and Príncipe (iso3=STP) added to visa_free Group 2 (30 days). The prior record did not include this nationality; it appeared on the embassy Kenya page as 'Sao Tomé e Príncipe' in Group 2.

CORRECTION 4: Puerto Rico removed from visa_free. Puerto Rico is a US territory; residents travel on US passports. It is not a separate nationality for passport classification purposes. Covered under United States of America (iso3=USA).
CORRECTION 5: Northern Mariana Islands removed from visa_free. Same rationale as Puerto Rico — US territory, residents travel on US passports.
CORRECTION 6: Taiwan iso3 changed from null to 'TWN'. Taiwan holds ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code TWN (assigned as 'Taiwan, Province of China'). The prior record set this to null.

CORRECTION 7: US/Canada credential condition notes corrected. The DGME text (migracion.go.cr/visas/) explicitly states the combined US visa type 'C1/D' IS accepted (it is a combined crew/transit-departure visa, not a pure transit visa). Only US visa types C1, C2, and C3 issued for transit-only purposes are excluded. The prior record stated 'excluding C1/C2/C3 transit visas' which was partially accurate but did not clarify the C1/D combined visa acceptance.

CORRECTION 8: All source_url fields updated from 'https://costaricanembassy.co.ke/visas/' to 'https://migracion.go.cr/visas/' for entries that could be verified on the DGME primary source. Honduras and São Tomé entries retain embassy Kenya URL as those were confirmed there.
CORRECTION 9: visitcostarica.com removed from official_domains — it is a tourism board site and not a government immigration domain.

UNCHANGED: RBI program amounts (USD 150,000 investor, USD 2,500 rentista, USD 1,000 pensionado) could not be re-verified directly from migracion.go.cr due to HTTP 403 on that subpage; figures retained from prior record as consistent with Law 9996 references.
gaps: Costa Rica's DGME website subpages (migracion.go.cr/informacion-general/, migracion.go.cr/nomadas-digitales/) return HTTP 403 Forbidden to direct WebFetch; main visa page (migracion.go.cr/visas/) accessible via Firecrawl stealth proxy.

Full Group 3 consular-visa country list (~80+ countries) confirmed from embassy Kenya page and cross-referenced with Fragomen/Tico Times reporting on Alcance N° 148; the DGME master list text on migracion.go.cr/visas/ does not enumerate countries inline (they reference the PDF at Alcance N° 148 which was also HTTP 403).
Pensionado minimum USD 1,000/month and Inversionista USD 150,000 figures retained from prior record; not re-verified directly from official DGME subpage due to HTTP 403.

Inversionista USD 150,000 sunset clause status: the regulation states this reduced amount may revert to USD 200,000 if not extended by the National Assembly; current status as of research date not confirmed from official page.
Honduras police clearance age threshold (18+) confirmed from Fragomen alert and embassy Kenya page but the exact article text from DGME was not fetched directly.

Dominican Republic visa status in Group 3 confirmed from embassy Kenya page; temporary maritime-entry exemption (Resolution D.JUR-0535-10-2024-ABM) exists for sea arrivals alongside Colombia and Ecuador — scope and current validity of that exemption not fully verified.
Japan and South Korea as credential issuers: not listed in DGME Alcance N° 148 text as confirmed from migracion.go.cr/visas/; excluded from conditional_access pending any future DGME update.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no official Costa Rican government source mentions OCI as a qualifying credential; India remains Group 3.
Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements: not verified from an accessible official Costa Rican government page.
Path to permanent residence (years before PR grant) for investor/rentista/pensionado not confirmed from official source; only naturalization citizenship timeline cited.

sources_checked.description: DGME official visa page – primary source
sources_checked.note: Accessible via Firecrawl stealth proxy (June 2026); HTTP 403 via direct WebFetch. Confirms Group 1 passport validity '1 día natural', credential exception categories, Group 2 passport validity '90 días naturales', Group 3/4 passport validity '180 días naturales'.

sources_checked.description: DGME official Directrices Generales de Visas – Alcance N° 148, La Gaceta N° 216, 17 November 2025
sources_checked.note: HTTP 403 Forbidden on both WebFetch and Firecrawl

sources_checked.description: Official Costa Rican Embassy in Kenya – full visa group lists and conditional access rules
sources_checked.note: Confirms Honduras in Group 2 with police clearance notation, São Tomé and Príncipe in Group 2, Group 1 passport validity shown as '1 month' (conservative vs. DGME's '1 day'). Embassy email concr-ke@rree.go.cr confirms official status.

sources_checked.description: Fragomen – Costa Rica/Honduras visa-free entry reinstated alert
sources_checked.note: Confirms Honduras reinstated to Group 2 effective October 26, 2023; Honduran nationals aged 18+ require police clearance certificate; stay up to 30 days renewable to 90.

sources_checked.description: Fragomen – summary of DGME resolution November 2025 (Alcance N° 148)
sources_checked.note: Confirms credential-based exemptions, Honduras reclassified to Group 2 under Alcance N° 148, Schengen visa multiple-entry requirement.

sources_checked.description: Costa Rica Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Worship
sources_checked.note: Confirmed bilateral agreements section and Russia visa suppression agreement.

outbound_official.note: no official Costa Rica government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Costa Rican Ministry of Foreign Affairs (rree.go.cr) Protocolo para Viajeros Costarricenses page explicitly redirects citizens to IATA (iatatravelcentre.com) or individual embassies/consulates for outbound visa requirements — it does not publish its own enumerated list. No alternative official Costa Rican government page (migracion.go.cr, rree.go.cr, cancilleria.go.cr) with a comprehensive outbound visa-free country list for ordinary Costa Rican passport holders was located as of June 2026.
